column3: Description
An iconic towering concrete monument celebrating Algeria's independence.
A lush and historic botanical garden established in 1832 featuring thousands of plant species.
A historic UNESCO World Heritage citadel characterized by its narrow winding streets and rich history.

column2: Great Mosque of Algiers (Djamaa el Djazaïr)
column3: A modern architectural marvel boasting the world's tallest minaret.
A stunning Catholic basilica from the 19th century offering panoramic views of the Bay of Algiers.
A fascinating museum of prehistory and ethnography housed in a beautifully restored Moorish villa.
One of the largest art museums in Africa featuring Algerian and European masterpieces.
A beautifully preserved Ottoman-era palace complex sitting right on the Mediterranean coast.
A historic mosque blending Moorish and Byzantine architecture located at the foot of the Casbah.
A magnificent neo-Moorish post office that serves as a central landmark in downtown Algiers.

column2: Museum of Antiquities and Islamic Arts
column3: The oldest museum in Algeria showcasing ancient mosaics and beautiful Islamic artifacts.
A bustling public square steeped in history and offering great views of the sea.
Located beneath the Martyrs' Memorial it details the Algerian struggle for independence.
An exquisite 18th-century Ottoman palace renowned for its interior tile work.

column2: Museum of Modern Art of Algiers (MaMa)
column3: A hub for contemporary Algerian art located in a beautifully restored neo-Moorish commercial building.
A 17th-century Ottoman mosque with striking white domes prominently situated near the harbor.

A sprawling zoo and amusement park perfect for family outings.
A quiet geometrical park offering scenic stairs greenery and Ottoman-inspired ceramics.

column3: Housed in a 16th-century palace this museum displays traditional Algerian crafts and jewelry.
One of the oldest surviving palaces in the Casbah featuring classic courtyard architecture.
A majestic former winter palace known for its fusion of Moorish and European styles.
A vibrant central plaza featuring a prominent statue of the famous Algerian leader.
The historic and modern maritime heart of the city.
A highly popular waterfront park area with walking paths playgrounds and sea views.
A picturesque marina and historical peninsula perfect for coastal dining and boat watching.

A spacious sandy beach offering numerous tourist complexes and relaxation spots.
A rocky and scenic coastal stretch known for excellent seafood restaurants.
The sprawling main venue for international trade fairs auto shows and major cultural events.
A striking modern cathedral built in the 1950s designed to look like a cooling tower or a nomad's tent.
A lush urban green space offering a tranquil escape from the bustling city.
A well-shaded family-friendly park known for its old trees and peaceful atmosphere.
A massive developing ecological park ideal for long walks and outdoor activities.

A fascinating museum located in the historic Amirauté building showcasing Algeria's rich naval history.
A stunning performing arts venue located in a historic building.
A historic mosque situated within the winding alleys of the Casbah.
Believed to be one of the oldest mosques in the Casbah area.
A 17th-century mosque built by an Italian convert with a fascinating backstory.

Originally the Great Synagogue of Algiers before being converted into a mosque.

The upscale center of the Hydra district known for chic cafes and restaurants.
A popular urban beach located in the Bab El Oued neighborhood.

The largest forest in the Algiers province offering excellent hiking trails.

The vibrant commercial artery of Algiers featuring stunning Haussmannian architecture.
A central and busy intersection famous for its bookstores and student life.

column2: Monument to the Dead of Algiers
column3: A solemn monument paying tribute to those who lost their lives in World War I.
